text: Writing clean code is an important skill for any programmer. Readable code is easy for others—and your future self—to understand. One key practice is using clear naming conventions; variable and function names should describe their purpose, like `calculateTotal()` instead of `doStuff()`. Another important idea is to keep functions small and focused on a single task. This makes the code easier to test and reuse. When you follow these habits, your code becomes more maintainable over time, meaning it is simpler to fix bugs or add new features without breaking everything.
